county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Howard. County seat: Keytesville. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Keytesville holds land records from 1820. Chariton County is one of Missouri's oldest counties, in the Missouri River 'Boonslick' region — the earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Sterling Price, Civil War general, was from Chariton County. Significant enslaved persons records in probate and tax records pre-1865. Chariton County Historical Society has genealogical files. Strong Virginia and Kentucky settlement heritage. Thomas Hart Benton owned land here. FamilySearch microfilm covers early deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1820

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1859 from Greene, Taney, Webster. County seat: Ozark. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Ozark holds land records from 1859. Christian County is adjacent to Springfield (Greene County) and has experienced significant suburban growth. The county was carved from Greene, Taney, and Webster — check parent counties for pre-1859 records. Christian County Historical Society and Museum in Ozark has family files. Ozark heritage with Southern settlement patterns. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1836 from Lewis. County seat: Kahoka.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Kahoka holds land records from 1836. Clark County is in northeast Missouri on the Des Moines River. Clark County Historical Society has genealogical collections. The Battle of Athens (1861), Missouri's northernmost Civil War battle, occurred here. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia. Mark Twain's family had connections to this area. Fox and Sauk Indian territory — check NARA for Indian agency records. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1822 from Ray. County seat: Liberty.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Liberty holds land records from 1822. Clay County is historically significant as the home of Jesse James (Kearney) and the site of the first daylight bank robbery (Liberty, 1866). William Jewell College in Liberty has local history collections. Clay County Museum and Historical Society maintains extensive genealogical files. The county had significant Mormon activity — Liberty Jail held Joseph Smith in 1838-1839. Strong Southern settlement with significant enslaved persons records. Kansas City suburban growth has transformed the county since the mid-20th century. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1833 from Clay. County seat: Plattsburg.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Plattsburg holds land records from 1833. Clinton County Historical Society has genealogical files. The county was on the frontier of American settlement in the 1830s. Plattsburg was an important stop on overland routes. Strong Southern settlement from Kentucky and Virginia. Some Civil War guerrilla activity but courthouse survived. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate records. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Check Clay County for related early settlement records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1820 from Cooper. County seat: Jefferson City. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Missouri German Belt — significant German immigrant settlement from the 1830s-1860s.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Jefferson City holds land records from 1820. Cole County contains Jefferson City, the state capital since 1826. Missouri State Archives in Jefferson City is the primary state repository for genealogical records — holding county records on microfilm, state government records, Confederate and Union service records, and vital records. The Missouri State Penitentiary (1836-2004) records document inmates and staff. Cole County Historical Society maintains local family files. German immigrant community was significant in Jefferson City. Lincoln University (founded 1866 for African Americans) records. FamilySearch microfilm is extensive for Cole County and statewide records held here.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1818 from Howard. County seat: Boonville. Historic Boonslick region — earliest area of American settlement west of St. Louis. Part of Missouri's 'Little Dixie' — strong Southern heritage and slaveholding region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Boonville holds land records from 1818. Cooper County is in the historic Boonslick region, among the earliest areas of American settlement in Missouri. Battle of Boonville (1861) was an early Civil War engagement. Cooper County Historical Society in Pilot Grove has genealogical collections. Kemper Military School records (1844-2002). Strong Southern heritage with significant enslaved persons records — Cooper County was a major slaveholding county. Boonville had German immigrant community. Missouri River steamboat landing records. FamilySearch microfilm covers early deed books and probate records from 1818.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1829 from Gasconade. County seat: Steelville. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement. Missouri Lead Mining District — lead mining from French colonial era to present.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Steelville holds land records from 1829. Crawford County is in the Ozark foothills along the Meramec River. Crawford County Historical Society has family files. Onondaga Cave and other natural features attracted early settlers. Iron mining and timber industries shaped the county's economy. Route 66 passed through Crawford County — 20th-century roadside tourism records. Strong Scots-Irish and Southern settlement. FamilySearch microfilm covers deed books and probate. Missouri State Archives holds circuit court records. Crawford was a parent county for several surrounding counties.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Barry, Polk. County seat: Greenfield.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Greenfield holds records restarting after the 1863 courthouse burning. Dade County suffered severe Civil War guerrilla destruction. For pre-1863 records, check Barry County and Polk County (parent counties), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO records. Dade County Historical Society in Greenfield has post-war family files. The county was on the border between Union and Confederate sympathies. Strong Southern settlement heritage. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Missouri Digital Heritage may have digitized surviving fragments.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO original land patents; county Recorder of Deeds records from 1841

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Missouri county formed 1841 from Polk. County seat: Buffalo. Ozark Highlands region with rugged terrain and Southern Appalachian settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Recorder of Deeds in Buffalo holds records restarting after the 1864 courthouse burning during the Civil War. For pre-1864 records, check Polk County (parent), Missouri State Archives, and BLM GLO land patents. Dallas County Historical Society in Buffalo maintains post-war genealogical files. Ozark county with Southern settlement heritage. Bennett Spring State Park area was early settlement zone. FamilySearch microfilm covers post-war records. Missouri State Archives holds some surviving circuit court records.
